skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative
skill.summary: Generate AI videos using 40+ models including Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok via the inference.sh CLI tool
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill provides legitimate AI video generation documentation using the belt CLI tool. The analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and misidentified 'Seedance' (an AI model name) as weak cryptographic code. Network and external command flags represent expected behavior for a CLI wrapper skill.

high_findings.title: False Positive: 'Weak Cryptographic Algorithm' Detection
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 and :63 as containing weak cryptographic algorithms. This is incorrect - the analyzer detected 'Seedance' (a ByteDance AI video model name) and misinterpreted table formatting as code. No cryptographic code exists in this file.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: 'Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution' Detection
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged multiple lines as Ruby shell execution with backticks. This is incorrect - the detected backticks are markdown code formatting syntax used throughout the documentation for inline code. No Ruby shell execution exists in this file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs for Service Endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill contains URLs to inference.sh services and documentation. These are legitimate service endpoints required for the skill's functionality. No credential exfiltration or unauthorized data transmission detected.

content.value_statement: Creating professional AI-generated videos requires navigating multiple APIs and model providers. This skill simplifies video generation by providing ready-to-use commands for Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and dozens of other AI video models through the inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate text-to-video clips using Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance, Wan, and Grok models
Animate images into videos with Wan 2.5 and other image-to-video models
Create talking avatar videos with OmniHuman and Fabric lipsync tools
Add sound effects and foley audio to videos with HunyuanVideo Foley
Upscale video quality using Topaz Video Upscaler
Merge multiple video clips with transitions
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and belt CLI installation
Video generation is asynchronous - results may take minutes to hours depending on model
Output quality depends on the underlying AI model and input parameters
Some models may have rate limits or credit requirements

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Creates Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Generate product demo videos and promotional clips by describing the scene you want, then use the AI to create realistic footage without expensive video production.

use_cases.title: Developer Builds Automated Video Pipeline
use_cases.description: Integrate AI video generation into content workflows using belt CLI commands that can be scripted and automated for batch video processing.

use_cases.title: Creator Produces AI Avatar Presentations
use_cases.description: Transform static images into talking avatar videos by combining image-to-video models with text-to-speech audio, perfect for explainer videos and tutorials.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video of {scene_description} using Veo 3.1 Fast model
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple video clip from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image {image_url} into a video using Wan 2.5 model
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have an image and want to bring it to life

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar video using the image at {image_url} and audio at {audio_url} with OmniHuman model
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a talking head video with voice sync

prompt_templates.title: Full Video Production Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a complete video production pipeline

output_examples.input: Generate a drone shot flying over mountains at sunset
output_examples.output: A 5-second cinematic video clip of a drone flying through mountain landscapes bathed in golden hour light, complete with motion blur on the rotors and sweeping panoramic views.

output_examples.input: Animate this portrait image into a talking head video
output_examples.output: A realistic talking head video where the portrait subject naturally moves and speaks, with proper lip sync, natural blinking, and subtle head movements.

output_examples.input: Add footsteps on gravel and rain sounds to this video
output_examples.output: The original video enhanced with synchronized sound effects - gravel crunching underfoot, distant thunder, and rain droplets hitting surfaces - mixed naturally with any existing audio.

best_practices: Be specific and descriptive in your video prompts - include camera angles, lighting conditions, and motion details for better results
Start with fast/lightweight models to test your prompts, then upgrade to higher quality models once satisfied with the results
Use consistent image dimensions and aspect ratios when combining image-to-video with existing content
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make a cool video' - AI video models need specific scene descriptions to generate coherent content
Avoid requesting unrealistic scenarios that contradict physics - most models generate physically plausible content
Do not assume instant results - video generation is compute-intensive and may require waiting for job completion

faq.question: What AI models are available for video generation?
faq.answer: The skill supports 40+ models including Google Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, and Fabric. Use 'belt app list --category video' to browse all available models.

faq.question: How do I install the belt CLI tool?
faq.answer: Follow the installation instructions at the provided GitHub link. After installation, run 'belt login' to authenticate with your inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can I generate videos from existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, use image-to-video models like Wan 2.5 by providing an image_url parameter. You can also create talking avatar videos with audio using OmniHuman or Fabric.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model complexity and queue. Fast models like Veo 3.1 Fast complete in minutes, while high-quality models may take longer. Check status with 'belt app status'.

faq.question: What video formats are supported for upscaling?
faq.answer: The Topaz Video Upscaler supports common formats including MP4, MOV, and AVI. Output resolutions depend on the source quality and model capabilities.

faq.question: Can I add audio to generated videos?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add sound effects using HunyuanVideo Foley with a text prompt describing the desired audio. For voice, use a separate text-to-speech skill to generate narration first.
